title: Hallelujah, What a Savior!

metadata.composer: 'Hallelujah What a Savior' Philip Paul Bliss, 1875
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Philip Paul Bliss, 1875. Music and Setting: 'Hallelujah What a Savior' Philip Paul Bliss, 1875.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.

1.raw_text: Man of Sorrows! what a name For the Son of God, who came Ruined sinners to reclaim. Hallelujah! What a Savior!

2.raw_text: Bearing shame and scoffing rude, In my place condemned He stood; Sealed my pardon with His blood.

3.raw_text: Guilty, vile, and helpless we; Spotless Lamb of God was He; "Full atonement!" can it be?

4.raw_text: Lifted up was He to die; "It is finished!" was His cry; Now in Heav'n exalted high.

5.raw_text: When He comes, our glorious King, All His ransomed home to bring, Then anew His song we'll sing:
